title: Temperature change
short: The difference between final and initial temperature, ΔT = T − T₀, in kelvin (K).
full:
A temperature change ΔT is the difference between a final and an initial temperature. Because the Kelvin and Celsius degrees are the same size, a change of 20 °C is identical to a change of 20 K — so you can use Celsius differences directly in Q = c·m·ΔT and ΔL = α·L₀·ΔT.

A positive ΔT means heating (and expansion); a negative ΔT means cooling (and contraction).

The SI unit is the kelvin (K).
